run.issue_title: [P1] Delete run-once and loop CLI commands — define the error out of existence

payload.summary: Removed the legacy `mix conductor run-once` and `mix conductor loop` entrypoints so they now print a removal message and exit 1, replaced internal `Orchestrator.start_loop/1` usage with `configure_polling/1`, updated orchestrator and CLI tests, added subprocess CLI exit tests, refreshed CLAUDE.md, and verified with `cd conductor && mix test` plus green PR checks.

findings.description: Run took 18+ minutes for a simple CLI command deletion. Workspace preparation (1.6s) was fine, but builder execution took 10+ minutes for what should be a 2-minute change. This suggests either sprite resource constraints or inefficient build processes.
findings.root_cause: Build environment not optimized for simple changes - full test suite or dependency resolution may be running unnecessarily
findings.title: Run duration indicates workspace preparation bottleneck

payload.summary: clean deletion run with performance concern

## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request refactors the Conductor application by eliminating outdated command-line interface entry points and modernizing the core orchestrator's initialization process. The changes aim to simplify the user experience by enforcing a single, consistent method for starting the conductor pipeline, thereby reducing confusion and maintaining a cleaner codebase. This streamlines the application's operational model and improves its maintainability. ### Highlights * **Deprecated CLI Commands Removed**: The `mix conductor run-once` and `mix conductor loop` CLI commands have been removed. Attempting to use these commands will now result in a clear error message and an exit status of 1, guiding users to the recommended `mix conductor start` command. * **Orchestrator API Update**: The internal orchestrator boot API `Conductor.Orchestrator.start_loop` has been replaced with `Conductor.Orchestrator.configure_polling` to align with the streamlined operational model. * **Documentation and Test Coverage Enhanced**: Documentation in `CLAUDE.md` has been updated to reflect the new operating model. New subprocess CLI tests have been added to verify the correct exit behavior for the removed commands, and existing orchestrator tests have been updated to use the new API.
Changelog * **CLAUDE.md** * Updated operating model section to reflect the removal of `run-once` and `loop` commands, replacing them with `start` and `pause`/`resume`. * **conductor/lib/conductor/application.ex** * Replaced `Conductor.Orchestrator.start_loop` with `Conductor.Orchestrator.configure_polling` for orchestrator initialization. * **conductor/lib/conductor/cli.ex** * Removed `run-once` and `loop` from the list of supported commands. * Introduced `removed_command` function to handle calls to deprecated commands, providing a message and exiting with status 1. * Removed `cmd_run_once`, `cmd_loop`, `warn_legacy_command`, `maybe_warn_label_deprecation`, and `maybe_warn_unfiltered_scope` functions. * Moved `Application.ensure_all_started(:conductor)` calls to specific command handlers. * **conductor/lib/conductor/orchestrator.ex** * Updated module documentation to remove references to `run_once` and `loop` modes. * Removed the `run_once` public function. * Renamed `start_loop` to `configure_polling` and updated its documentation. * Removed private functions `run_issue` and `find_latest_run`. * **conductor/test/conductor/cli_fleet_test.exs** * Removed `RunOnceTracker` and `RunOnceLauncher` mock modules. * Removed environment variable configurations for `orig_tracker` and `orig_launcher`. * Deleted tests related to the deprecated `loop` and `run-once` commands. * **conductor/test/conductor/cli_legacy_command_test.exs** * Added new test file. * Added tests to verify that `mix conductor run-once` and `mix conductor loop` commands print a removal message and exit with status 1. * **conductor/test/conductor/orchestrator_test.exs** * Updated comments and test descriptions to reference `configure_polling` instead of `start_loop`. * Removed tests specifically for the `run_once/1` function. * Replaced all calls to `Orchestrator.start_loop` with `Orchestrator.configure_polling`.
Activity * phrazzld created the pull request with a clear summary of the changes, including the removal of deprecated CLI entrypoints, the internal API replacement, and the addition of new CLI tests. * phrazzld provided detailed testing instructions for verifying the changes.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG). You can also get AI-powered code generation, chat, as well as code reviews directly in the IDE at no cost with the [Gemini Code Assist IDE Extension](https://cloud.google.com/products/gemini/code-assist).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request refactors the Conductor application by removing the `run-once` and `loop` commands and their underlying implementation. The previous `start_loop` functionality in the `Orchestrator` module has been renamed to `configure_polling` to align with a new operating model that uses a `start` command with a `fleet.toml` configuration, alongside `pause` and `resume` commands for dispatch control. The changes include updating CLI commands, application logic, documentation, and tests to reflect these removals and refactorings, with new tests added to confirm the deprecation messages for the removed commands.

pr.title: fix: remove deprecated conductor run commands
